description: Evaluates privileged access management service maturity—PAM systems, JIT and ephemeral privileged access, KPIs/KRIs, unified application and device privileged access, service custodianship, and SOC logging with session recording.

capabilities.name: Service exists and is in good condition
capabilities.description: A privileged access management service exists and is reviewed on a regular cadence for efficacy.

capabilities.name: PAM system
capabilities.description: A PAM system manages privileged accounts enterprise-wide with lifecycle automation, JIT access support, configuration review, and integration with applications, endpoints, and network devices.
evaluation_focus: PAM system deployed for privileged accounts
Automated PAM user lifecycle and access
Integration with applications, endpoints, and network devices
Just-in-time privileged access capability in PAM platform

capabilities.name: KPIs/KRIs
capabilities.description: PAM KPIs and KRIs—including standing privilege ratio, JIT adoption, and session recording coverage—are documented and reviewed at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: Standing versus just-in-time privileged access metrics

capabilities.name: Privileged access provisioning
capabilities.description: Privileged access to application and endpoint resources is formalized, unified across assets, automated via self-service portals, and reviewed at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: Unified privileged access across application and endpoint assets

capabilities.name: Just-in-time and ephemeral privileged access
capabilities.description: Standing privileged access is minimized; time-bound, approval-gated, and ephemeral elevation is used for administrative tasks with automatic session expiry.
evaluation_focus: Policy to minimize standing privileged access
Automatic revocation of ephemeral privileged sessions
Break-glass privileged access with enhanced logging

capabilities.name: Privilege account service custodian
capabilities.description: A dedicated PAM service manager owns privileged accounts rather than the IAM engineer or custodian.
evaluation_focus: Separation from IAM engineer or custodian ownership

capabilities.name: PAM logging and session recording
capabilities.description: Privileged account activity, session recordings, and lifecycle changes are logged securely to the SOC, including dynamic changes and anomalous privileged activity.
evaluation_focus: Documented PAM activity logging to SOC
Secure privileged account logging to SOC
Dynamic privileged account changes to SOC

evaluation.resiliency_weight_policy: Ask stakeholder for per-capability weights when adding a new evaluation service. This service uses weight 1 for all capabilities.
evaluation.pillar_policy: Some services may have no Technology capabilities; in those cases, do not compute or report a Technology pillar score.
